title: Hartbeeps
description:
Join Dexter Cat & Adrian Mouse in the Hartbeeps Happy House from 10am. Participants take a different sound journey every week, as we set the scene and your Little Hartbeepers build their story! It’s fun fun fun all the way, with our own original versions of classic nursery rhymes, the most fabulous songs, gorgeous puppet characters, dress-up, props and more…

Happy House (for toddlers and their younger siblings)

All aboard the Baby Beeps Babble Express, next stop Babble Station! Sessions offer BIG fun in full colour, with larger than life puppet characters, rhymes, raps, claps and rhythmic beats; supporting baby’s blossoming speech and language development.

Baby Bells classes are designed to ignite and delight those freshly acquired senses and help baby to uncurl and release trapped energy. Sessions focus on bonding and building confidence.

Hartbeeps are currently running a promotion for babies under 8 weeks to join us for free in our Baby Bells class at 12:30, using the promo code UNDER8WEEKS to claim.
address: Stanley Arts, 12 Stanley Road, South Norwood, London, SE25 4RJ

title: Drawing Ground: The Pause with Beyond Space
description:
How can we make our lives more creative and joyful? Perhaps by setting aside one creative day each month as a starting point.

Drawing is a simple and powerful way to reconnect with your imagination, slow down, and reset. Drawing Ground: The Pause at Stanley Arts is a series of relaxed and welcoming drawing sessions for adults, offering a gentle creative space to pause, breathe, and make art away from the pressures of everyday life.

This is for anyone needing a creative reset – whether you’re a freelancer taking a mindful day away from work, a parent carving out a rare quiet moment, an older adult exploring creativity with more time, or simply someone looking for calm, inspiration, and connection.

Each session explores a different approach to drawing, with gentle guidance, creative prompts, and all materials provided. Inspired by the performative energy of Stanley Arts, these sessions invite you to explore drawing as a sensory experience. You’ll be invited to explore drawing in playful, imaginative, and sometimes unexpected ways. No previous experience is needed. You’ll leave with your own unique drawings, a sense of creative confidence, and a refreshed mind.

Refreshments will be provided. Enjoy a morning tea or coffee while meeting others and drawing together.

By the end of these sessions, you will leave with a set of refined drawings and new ideas about drawing that you can continue to explore in your own way.

Session 1, 22 May: Close observation & sensory still life drawing

Starting with a series of guided drawing exercises, participants will explore drawing as a way of sensing – paying attention to detail, texture, and rhythm from provided objects. The focus is on slowing perception and translating what is seen and felt into your drawing.

Session 2, 26 June: Expressive pastel drawing inspired by abstract artists

Participants will create an expressive drawing using soft pastels, working with movement and gesture beyond the desk. Inspired by abstract artists, you explore how colour, rhythm, and mark-making can express energy and feeling.

Session 3, 17 July: Fluid watercolour drawing

Introducing watercolour as a fluid extension of drawing, participants will explore wet drawing materials responding intuitively rather than controlling outcomes. You combine line, wash, and chance effects to create atmospheric works.
address: Stanley Arts, 12 Stanley Road, South Norwood, London, SE25 4RJ

title: Cherrystars Performing Arts
description:
Cherrystars are an award winning, community performing arts school. Their team consist of BRIT School alumni and professional television and theatre actors who specialise in teaching innovative performing arts classes to the early years.

Star in their magical movies and stage shows building confidence, creativity and community!

The core values at Cherrystars are Communication, Creativity, Confidence Building and Community. They have taken great pride over the last decade, to have provided a safe, nurturing and affirming place in the local community for children to unleash their creativity and develop a love of the arts.

Visit the Cherrystars website today.

Follow Cherrystars on Instagram, Facebook & X (FKA Twitter) for the latest news.
address: Stanley Arts, 12 Stanley Road, South Norwood, London, SE25 4RJ

title: Annie Jr the Musical
description: Cherrystars Stage and Screen School presents Annie Jr. The Musical, the timeless story of one little girl’s unshakeable optimism in the face of a hard-knock life. Performed by an extraordinary cast of young performers, this production promises big voices, big heart, and a whole lot of joy.
address: Stanley Arts, 12 Stanley Road, South Norwood, London, SE25 4RJ

title: The June Stanley Arts Seasonal Supper Club
description:
The June Stanley Arts’ Seasonal Supper Club, Saturday 27th June 2026 will be holiday themed with a six-course menu taking inspiration from holiday and seaside culinary classics.

The menu, created and presented by local food writer, Liz O’Keefe, will be revealed on the evening. The Stanley Arts’ Seasonal Supper Club is a slow and delicious evening of savouring homemade, locally produced and/or homegrown food. Drinks can be purchased from the theatre bar in advance, along with your ticket, and there is a corkage option if you fancy bringing a particular bottle.

You can choose from pescatarian, vegetarian or vegan when you book, and please let us know of any major food allergies when booking and they will be accommodated.

Liz has been running the Stanley Arts’ Seasonal Supper Club for a year now and previously ran a monthly supper club at Brown and Green cafe, Crystal Palace, as well a quarterly supper club in Aldgate.
address: Stanley Arts, 12 Stanley Road, South Norwood, London, SE25 4RJ

title: Boxed In
description:
A quirky, thought-provoking exploration of the unspoken rules that shape our lives.

Meet Derek and Jon, two friends trying to understand life’s strange instructions. When “stop” means “go”, “up” turns into “down” and no one explains why – what do you do? Everyone else seems to know the way, but what if you just don’t get it?

Boxed In is a playful and thought-provoking journey about feeling left out, not because you’ve done something wrong, but because the world doesn’t always play fair. It’s a story for anyone who’s ever felt a bit confused, a bit left out, or a bit boxed in.

Through movement, visual storytelling, humour and a touch of chaos, Boxed In explores the invisible rules of behaviour, the way people act and what others think you should do – and invites us all to imagine a world where everyone feels they belong.

A co-production by Daryl & Co. and Half Moon, Boxed In is an exciting collaboration between a ground-breaking Disabled-led theatre company and the UK’s leading small-scale young people’s venue and touring company.

Boxed In is largely non-verbal. This makes it accessible to a wide range of people, including d/Deaf, hard of hearing, BSL users and non-English speakers.  Accessible. Inclusive. Made for everyone.
address: Stanley Arts, 12 Stanley Road, South Norwood, London, SE25 4RJ

title: Mini Movers & Their Grown-up Groovers
description:
These family dance workshops offer a fun and creative opportunity for children and their grownups to dance and move together!

This dance workshop will explore togetherness through moving, using contact, props and drawing and offers a great family bonding experience.

Suitable for children 12 months – 4 years old and their grown-ups!

This is a class for children and their grown-ups to dance together! Children must therefore be accompanied by an adult for the duration of the class. Due to the popularity of these classes, tickets for additional accompanying adults are charged at £6, please book.

The class is led by professional dance teacher Rachele Rapisardi. Find out more about Rachele’s practice below…

Rachele RapisaVrdi is a dance artist who for 12 years has been working across the UK, Europe and Israel with companies such as Lost Dog (Ben Duke), ilDance, Protein Dance (Luca Silvestrini), Clod Ensemble (Suzy Wilson and Paul Clark) and Russel Maliphant.

After obtaining an MA in Performing Arts in 2015 her freelance career expanded to include the roles of teacher, co-creator, Rehearsal Director and Associate Director for the above companies, with whom I have kept strong relationships throughout the years.

In the last six years Rachele has focused her practice particularly on collaborative making and community dance work.

As a teacher and creator she has worked with both professionals and dance students (from GCSE to MA) as well as leading artist on community based projects with refugee centers, pupils in referral units, care homes and families.

£6 tickets must be purchased for each child attending the class. Multiple accompanying adults are welcome to join, while one is included with your one child booking, please be sure to book your £6, ‘Additional accompanying Adult’ ticket.

The class commences at 11am, so please arrive for then.

Class Duration: 45 minutes

If you have any access requirements, please give us a call on 020 8251 0184 or email us on bookings@stanleyarts.org and we will be happy to help!
address: Stanley Arts, 12 Stanley Road, South Norwood, London, SE25 4RJ

title: June Family Craft: Summer Frames
description:
Ella Yarde is back in the building to lead another crafty family Sunday at Stanley Arts!

Join us for a fun, family-friendly workshop where you’ll create your own unique photo frames using recycled materials! Get creative with a variety of textures and shapes as you transform everyday items into something special and personal. Perfect for all ages, this hands-on session is a great way to explore creativity while giving new life to old materials. All materials are included—just bring your imagination!

If you have any access requirements, please give us a call on 020 8251 0184 or email us on bookings@stanleyarts.org and we will be happy to help.

This is a drop in event – tickets are valid for arrival at any time from the start of the session. Attendees are encouraged to arrive at least 20 minutes before the end of the session to have enough time to engage with the activity.

Suitable for families and children ages 3+.
address: Stanley Arts, 12 Stanley Road, South Norwood, London, SE25 4RJ

title: Matt Forde and Sara Barron – Work in Progress
description:
Join Matt Forde and Sara Barron as they work up their shows before heading up to the Edinburgh Fringe.

Matt Forde fresh from wrapping up his biggest tour yet and a second series of The Matt Forde Focus Group on BBC R4, leading political comedian and satirist Matt Forde (The Royal Variety Performance, Spitting Image, HIGNFY, British Scandal) embarks on a mission with impossible odds: find joy amid global political turmoil, the rising tide of populism, and his own ongoing health challenges – several years on from a bone cancer diagnosis.

Sara Barron is one of the fastest-rising comedians of her generation. Since her 2018 debut at the Edinburgh Fringe, she’s starred on shows including Live at the Apollo (BBC), Would I Lie to You? (BBC) and Frankie Boyle’s New World Order (BBC).
address: Stanley Arts, 12 Stanley Road, South Norwood, London, SE25 4RJ

title: Technical Theatre Work Experience, Summer 2026
description:
Our technical theatre work experience programme is back!

Are you interested in a career in theatre; music; festivals and galleries, aged 18-25? The technical theatre work experience course gives you hands-on experience at a range of Stanley Arts shows and activities to learn about lighting; sound; stage management and event operations.

Our Technical Theatre Work Experience programme gives young people (18-25) an opportunity to work with our technical team to gain experience in lighting; sound; theatre space management, and events. 5 young people will have a training day with the in-house senior technician, and will then be able to select shifts independently throughout August-September to work alongside the team on some of our events & festivals.

Applications for our technical theatre work experience programme will open in May. If you would like to find out more information about the process prior to, please contact andrew@stanleyarts.org.
address: Stanley Arts, 12 Stanley Road, South Norwood, London, SE25 4RJ
